source_jobs.title: Director, Strategy and Operations
source_jobs.description:
Director, Strategy & Operations

 


ABOUT THE POSITION

Skimmer builds the software the powers the pool and spa industry. Thousands of pool professionals run their businesses on our platform. We’re a PE-backed vertical SaaS company in high-growth mode, scaling our operating maturity. This is a high-trust, high-leverage role for someone who moves fluidly between strategy and execution — and who keeps the company’s most important priorities moving.

You’ll act as an extension of the CFO and CEO and a close partner to the full leadership team — working alongside leaders who own their functions. The work is weighted toward strategy, M&A, exit readiness, and operational rigor, with program management as the throughline. You’ll report to our CFO with a dotted line to our CEO, be involved with active strategic projects from day one, and take on more decision-making over time as context and trust build.




ABOUT YOU:

You're a strategic operator who makes complex, cross-functional work move. You've earned trust in high-stakes rooms and know how to get the right people aligned and decisions made. You may not be a finance person, you bring strong commercial and analytical instincts and are fluent with financial models.

You’re the person who maps the workstream before anyone else has even named the problem. You build trust quickly, operate with discretion, and have a low tolerance for things falling through the cracks. You thrive in ambiguity, move fast without creating chaos, and bring the kind of calm, clear-headed coordination that makes executives breathe easier.

You’re proactive, low-ego, and solutions-oriented. You hold the whole team to commitments — including the CFO and CEO — and reassess early when things slip, rather than letting missed deadlines pass quietly.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

M&A & Integration

 - Project-manage active M&A end to end — diligence coordination, workstream tracking, and post-close integration — driving milestones and value capture alongside the CFO and relevant leaders

 - Serve as the connective tissue between deal teams and the broader organization across Legal, People, Finance, and Product — ensuring nothing gets lost in translation

 - Maintain centralized tracking of milestones and blockers; surface issues early and drive them to resolution

Strategic Planning

 - Run the annual and quarterly planning cadence — structure the analysis, frame choices with the leadership team, and turn decisions into a tracked operating plan with clear owners and dates

 - Track progress against strategic initiatives; flag misalignment early and facilitate the right conversations to keep priorities on course

 - Help the leadership team think through sequencing, resourcing, and cross-functional dependencies on key initiatives

Value Creation

 - Drive the company’s value-creation roadmap, keeping cross-functional readiness work moving toward long-term goals

 - Keep the value creation roadmap live and visible — ensuring progress is tracked and owners are held accountable across functions

Evaluation of Strategic Initiatives

 - Pressure-test and prioritize proposed and in-flight initiatives with their owners

 - Build the business cases and analysis — including new-market assessments for the CEO — that inform where the company places its bets

Cross-Functional Execution

 - Drive the company’s most important cross-functional initiatives to completion; maintain a single source of truth for top priorities with clear owners and tracked milestones

 - Run the leadership cadence as decision forums, not status meetings — log decisions and drive follow-through

 - Hold the team accountable to commitments and timelines — working alongside an empowered leadership team, not running their functions

Operational Maturity

 - Build the reporting discipline, processes, and operating rigor that scale the business and strengthen long-term enterprise value

 - Tighten reporting cadence, improve KPI reliability, and catch variance-to-pl

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant/Event Manager
source_jobs.description:
Skimmer http://getskimmer.com is on a mission: to modernize the pool and spa service and repair industry. We’re a private equity-backed company with 7000+ customers using Skimmer’s pool service platform to transform their businesses. But we’re not stopping there (there are over 70,000 pool service companies in this surprisingly large “niche” space).

Our customers love us almost as much as we love them (check out our reviews https://www.capterra.com/p/177014/Skimmer/reviews/ and our 50+ NPS score!)

We are currently #844 on the Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing companies (#92 in Texas and #84 in Software!), on the Deloitte Fast 500, one of Built In Austin's Best Startups, ABJ’s 6th Fastest Growing Company, and most importantly one of Austin's Best Places for Working Parents. We’re proud of the incredible company we’ve built, and we’re just getting started.

We’re looking for big thinkers with small egos, so let’s dive in!


AT SKIMMER WE

 - Realize our customers' success is our success.

 - Build humble, diverse teams who have fun winning.

 - Drive results with urgency.

 - Earn trust through transparent communication.




WHO ARE YOU? / ABOUT YOU:

Do you love helping companies run efficiently by doing whatever is needed? We promise that no day will be the same, so if you like variety, want to learn from our Executive Leadership Team, and take pride in making sure your team has everything they need, this job is for you.

Our ideal candidate will be proactive, adaptable, and able to manage multiple tasks efficiently with a positive attitude. You will be the point person for managing our CEO and CFO’s calendars and interactions with customers, Board members, and investors. You will also be the point person, brainchild, and executor of multiple Skimmer events, and an upholder of our company culture. You are someone who is proactive, able to juggle multiple initiatives, and a builder of relationships internally and externally. You’re a person people love working with.

This role, based in Austin, TX, reports to our VP, People, and supports our Executive Leadership Team.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Administrative Support:
   
   - Assist the executive team with scheduling meetings, appointments, and travel arrangements.
   
   - Prioritize and manage multiple calendars to ensure time efficiency.
   
   - Coordinate internal and external meetings, ensuring all logistical details are handled.
   
   - Maintain confidentiality while ensuring organization and security around confidential data and files.
   
   - Be a point person for administrative projects- assisting with some, delegating tasks with others. You are a person who “knows everything” and is always able to help.
   
   - Proactively assess executive needs and reach out to assist to avoid bottlenecks.

 - Events Management:
   
   - Own the planning, organizing, and execution of company events for our 115+ person team, host online and in-person team-building activities, and act as the point person for corporate meetings.
   
   - Bring creativity and fun to all Skimmer events- internal and external- with the goal of delighting customers, investors, and employees.
   
   - Manage vendor relationships, including negotiation of terms, invoicing, and performance tracking.
   
   - Work with cross-functional teams to ensure branding, invitations, and agendas are well coordinated and aligned to the event’s goals.

 - People Team Responsibilities:
   
   - Work with People Team members to create delightful candidate and employee experiences, including scheduling, welcoming, and follow-up communications.
   
   - Serve as backup for Office Management and HR Operations work, as appropriate
   
   - Manage Executive Team goals and waterfall goals for their teams to ensure transparency and tracking across Skimmer
     
     


WHAT YOU BRING:

 - Proven experience as an Executive Assistant or Administrative Assistant, Project/Events Manager, or similar role in a fast-paced, tech-foc

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Skimmer http://getskimmer.com is on a mission: to modernize the pool and spa service and repair industry. We’re a private equity-backed company with 7000+ customers using Skimmer’s pool service platform to transform their businesses. But we’re not stopping there (there are over 70,000 pool service companies in this surprisingly large “niche” space).

Our customers love us almost as much as we love them (check out our reviews https://www.capterra.com/p/177014/Skimmer/reviews/ and our NPS score of 68!)

We were recently honored to join the Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing companies, the Deloitte Fast 500, were named one of Built In Austin's Best Startups, as well as Austin's Best Places for Working Parents.

We’re looking for big thinkers with small egos, so let’s dive in!




OUR VALUES: AT SKIMMER WE

 - Realize our customers' success is our success.

 - Build humble, diverse teams who have fun winning.

 - Drive results with urgency.

 - Earn trust through transparent communication.


BEFORE APPLYING, PLEASE REVIEW THE BELOW REQUIRED LOCATIONS FOR REMOTE AND HYBRID EMPLOYMENT AT SKIMMER:

Skimmer has downtown office spaces in Austin, TX and Toronto, ON, and remote-first employees around the US. Right now we can hire hybrid employees in Austin and Toronto, or remote employees in the following states: Alabama, California, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Maryland, Michigan, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, Rhode Island, Tennessee, Texas, and Virginia.




OUR VALUES: AT SKIMMER WE

 - Realize our customers' success is our success.

 - Build humble, diverse teams who have fun winning.

 - Drive results with urgency.

 - Earn trust through transparent communication.




WHO ARE YOU? / ABOUT YOU

We are seeking an experienced Senior Product Manager to lead the continued development and evolution of our product roadmap. As a product leader, you will shape the roadmap, partner cross-functionally, and drive delivery of customer value in order to help pool professionals manage their daily work in the field and grow their business.

You’ll work closely with design, engineering, customer success, and leadership to continue to grow our world-class platform. Ensuring that it remains intuitive, reliable, and indispensable to our users. You will own the full lifecycle of product development, from discovery to delivery and adoption to iteration.

Reporting to the VP of Product, this role can be in Austin, TX or remote.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Own the product vision and strategy, aligning it with Skimmer’s overall business goals and user needs.

 - Define and prioritize the roadmap, collaborating with stakeholders to balance innovation, tech debt, and user feedback.

 - Lead product discovery efforts, including user research, customer interviews, and prototype testing to identify new opportunities and refine existing features.

 - Write clear, actionable requirements for design and engineering, and manage sprint cycles with an eye toward fast, high-quality execution.

 - Analyze product usage and customer feedback to measure success, validate decisions, and iterate as needed.

 - Act as a domain expert, keeping tabs on industry trends, emerging technologies, and competitor offerings.

 - Ensure a cohesive, delightful customer experience that supports Skimmer’s mission and brand.


WHAT PERFORMANCE LOOKS LIKE:

 - Within your first 3 months you will analyze and assess customer needs around our products and translate those into roadmap and business requirements for our UX and engineering team. You’ll have a deep understanding of our current web and mobile platforms and roadmap. You will interact with our customers and will lead efforts to ensure customer experience remains the best in the industry.




WHAT YOU BRING:

 - Experience leading development teams building native web applications including taking at least one product from concept-to-launch successfully.

 - Proven success leading end-to-end product devel

source_jobs.title: Principal Software Engineer- Partner Channels
source_jobs.description:
Skimmer http://getskimmer.com is on a mission: to modernize the pool and spa service and repair industry. We’re a private equity-backed company with 6000+ customers using Skimmer’s pool service platform to transform their businesses. But we’re not stopping there (there are over 70,000 pool service companies in this surprisingly large “niche” space).

Our customers love us almost as much as we love them (check out our reviews https://www.capterra.com/p/177014/Skimmer/reviews/ and our NPS score of 68!)

We were recently honored to join the Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing companies, the Deloitte Fast 500, were named one of Built In Austin's Best Startups, as well as Austin's Best Places for Working Parents.

We’re looking for big thinkers with small egos, so let’s dive in!

Our Values: At Skimmer we

 - Realize our customers' success is our success.

 - Build humble, diverse teams who have fun winning.

 - Drive results with urgency.

 - Earn trust through transparent communication.




BEFORE APPLYING, PLEASE REVIEW THE BELOW REQUIRED LOCATIONS FOR REMOTE AND HYBRID EMPLOYMENT AT SKIMMER:

Skimmer has downtown office spaces in Austin, TX and Toronto, ON, and remote-first employees around the US. Right now we can hire hybrid employees in Austin and Toronto, or remote employees in the following states: Alabama, California, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Maryland, Michigan, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, Rhode Island, Tennessee, Texas, and Virginia.




ABOUT THE POSITION:

Skimmer is looking for a player/coach technical leader to guide our Embedded Software Development team. You’ll lead a small team while contributing code that expands our platform through partner integrations, including embedded financial products and other third-party offerings that make Skimmer more valuable for customers.

This team owns the partner integration and enablement processes for the Skimmer platform and mobile app. Skimmer has a team that owns the customer facing API and middleware tools. This team specifically owns partner integrations that are deeply integrated into Skimmer. Many of these partners will be tightly integrated into the interface for financial services we resell.

You’ll report to the VP of Engineering and work closely with Product, Finance, Customer Success, and Support.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Lead and mentor a small team of development engineers while contributing technically as a player/coach.

 - Serve as the internal technical expert on Skimmer’s partner integration architecture, including embedded financial products and third-party offerings.

 - Drive evaluation, integration, and ongoing support for embedded products to ensure reliability, data accuracy, and a seamless customer experience.

 - Build and maintain secure APIs, webhooks, middleware, and integration patterns for partner functionality.

 - Partner on vendor selection and technical due diligence for prospective integration partners.

 - Use AI tools and best practices to improve team productivity and code quality.

 - Own security, compliance, and data privacy considerations across partner integrations.

First 90 days

 - Lead the build-out of the first embedded financial product integration with Product and the selected partner.

 - Define a reusable integration framework for auth, webhooks, retries, monitoring, and data sync.

 - Review and strengthen the security, compliance, and data-sharing posture for third-party integrations.




WHAT YOU BRING:

 - Tenured experience leading a development team in a technical leadership or player/coach role at a SaaS startup.

 - Strong hands-on experience building API-based, real-time integrations for SaaS products.

 - Deep knowledge of REST APIs, webhooks, OAuth, retries, idempotency, monitoring, and failure handling.

 - Experience with embedded financial products or similar third-party offerings is a strong plus.

 - Proficiency using AI tools, preferably Claude, to improve development

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for an experienced Data Engineer to help build and maintain the data models and customer-facing reports that power the Skimmer product. You'll own the data flows and transformations that feed analytics our customers rely on every day, and you'll build the embedded reporting experiences that help pool professionals understand and grow their businesses. This role spans data platform work and customer-facing analytics, so you'll partner closely with product, engineering, and business stakeholders. We're looking for someone who brings hands-on depth and sound judgment, and who can operate with a high degree of autonomy.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and maintain the data models and transformations that power reporting in the Skimmer product, leveraging tools like Fivetran transformations, dbt, and Sigma Computing materializations

 - Build and maintain customer-facing data models and reports in Sigma Computing, embedded within the Skimmer application

 - Manage and extend ingestion using Fivetran across a growing set of sources

 - Develop and maintain data transformations in Python and SQL against our Snowflake data warehouse

 - Partner with product and engineering to turn customer needs into reliable, performant reporting experiences

 - Collaborate with application engineers to understand source systems and ensure clean, reliable data capture

 - Monitor data quality, reliability, and report performance, and troubleshoot issues as they arise

 - Document data models, definitions, and architecture to support a growing team


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Disciplined Problem-Solving: Proven ability to decompose complex business problems into structured, executable plans. You prioritize understanding the 'why' and 'how' of data before implementation.

 - Proactive Communication: Comfortable identifying ambiguity and proactively seeking clarity from stakeholders rather than making assumptions.

 - Ownership of Quality: A strong sense of ownership—you don't consider a task 'done' until it is validated, performant, and meets the documented requirements.

 - Demonstrated experience in data engineering, with a track record of owning data infrastructure end to end

 - Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with a cloud data warehouse (Snowflake preferred)

 - Proficiency in Python for data transformation and automation

 - Experience working with C#/.NET application environments and partnering with application engineers on source systems

 - Experience with managed ingestion and transformation tooling (Fivetran, dbt, or similar)

 - Hands-on experience building data models and reports in a BI/analytics platform (Sigma Computing a strong plus), ideally embedded in a customer-facing product

 - Proficiency with AI coding tools like Claude or Cursor to work efficiently and raise your output

 - Solid understanding of data modeling concepts and ELT/ETL best practices

 - Strong communication skills and comfort working with both technical and non-technical partners


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with embedded analytics or customer-facing reporting at scale

 - Experience building data products in a SaaS or product-led company

 - Experience with rigorous code review processes and contributing to maintaining high engineering standards within a team.


BENEFITS

 - Competitive base pay + bonus potential

 - Generous medical, dental, and vision plans (we pay 100% of your premium and 50% of your dependents’)

 - Fantastic culture with a very strong eNPS- we work hard, celebrate wins, and learn as we go to always be improving to benefit our customers.

 - Immediate access to 401(k) with company match

 - Flexible PTO (MINIMUM of 10 days required every year)

 - 12 weeks paid parental leave for birthing parent, 6 weeks paid leave for supporting partner

 - Remote work friendly

 - Comprehensive learning and development budget

 - A manager dedicated to your development



At Skimmer we are deeply committed to building a diverse and inc

source_jobs.title: Account Executive  (Multi-Product)
source_jobs.description:
Account Executive (Multi-Product)

Skimmer http://getskimmer.com is on a mission: to modernize the pool and spa service and repair industry. We’re a private equity-backed company with 7000+ customers using Skimmer’s pool service platform to transform their businesses. But we’re not stopping there (there are over 70,000 pool service companies in this surprisingly large “niche” space).

Our customers love us almost as much as we love them (check out our reviews https://www.capterra.com/p/177014/Skimmer/reviews/ and our NPS score of 68!)

We were recently honored to join the Inc. 5000 list of fastest-growing companies, the Deloitte Fast 500, were named one of Built In Austin's Best Startups, as well as Austin's Best Places for Working Parents.

We’re looking for big thinkers with small egos, so let’s dive in!


OUR VALUES: AT SKIMMER WE

 - Realize our customers' success is our success.

 - Build humble, diverse teams who have fun winning.

 - Drive results with urgency.

 - Earn trust through transparent communication.


WHO ARE YOU?

We’re looking for an Account Executive (Multi-Product) who is a proven salesperson, ideally in SaaS, who thrives in full-cycle selling—sourcing, qualifying, and closing deals end-to-end. You’re naturally curious with strong discovery and analytical skills, and you know how to translate customer needs into clear solutions. Strong organizational skills and pipeline discipline are a must, and experience with HubSpot or another CRM will help you hit the ground running. You’re not afraid to pick up the phone and you believe that making an extra 10 calls is the difference between hitting and missing quota. Above all, you’re a kind human who easily adapts to change and embraces the opportunity to help shape how we sell and scale new financial products.

 


THE TEAM

You will be joining our Expansion Sales team, who sells our payments products and all new products through Skimmer such as our new AI phone assistant, bookkeeping, and more. All team members are remote (although Austin-based folks try to come into the office at least once a week) and you will be joining a company with a collaborative culture, focus on customers, and emphasis on having a good time together! 🏊




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

As one of the first Account Executives selling Skimmer's new multi-product solutions, you'll own the full sales cycle across a rapidly expanding product suite (currently, AIPhone, Account/Bookkeeping software, Marketing Suite and Payroll software) . This is a role where Account Executive meets Account Manager: you'll partner to win net-new customers, expand within our existing base, and run upsell and cross-sell motions across both.

You're getting in early, which means you'll help define how we continue to sell these products and you'll grow as our lineup does, because we'll be launching new solutions quickly.

 

1. Customer Outreach & Discovery

 - Engage and qualify both prospective customers and existing Skimmer Pros, tailoring your approach to where each one sits in their relationship with us.

 - Drive high-impact discovery conversations that dig beneath the surface to uncover core pain points, goals, and opportunities, recognizing that discovery is the most critical stage of the buying process.

 - Stay close to a fast-moving product roadmap so you can speak credibly to what's available today and what's coming next.

2. Sales Execution

 - Deliver powerful, tailored demonstrations of our multi-product solutions with the explicit goal of attaching payments to every customer account.

 - Act as a business consultant—analytically connecting customer pain points to our solutions, aligning stakeholders, and uncovering needs customers may not even realize they have.

 - Own the sale from start to finish: handle objections with creativity and confidence, involve the right decision-makers, and relentlessly drive toward closing deals, with average deal sizes ranging from $$$ to $$$ annually

3. Pipeline Growth & Manage

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Representative
source_jobs.description:
💦 ABOUT SKIMMER

At Skimmer, we’re on a mission to modernize the pool and spa service industry. More than 6,000 pool pros rely on our platform to manage and grow their businesses — and we’re just getting started.

We’re a private equity–backed, fast-growing SaaS company made up of big thinkers with small egos. Our software powers a surprisingly large niche of 70,000+ pool companies, and our customers love us almost as much as we love them (our reviews say it all).

If you’re passionate about helping people, thrive in a fast-paced environment, and want to join a team that genuinely cares about what they do — come make a splash with us.

 


💪 WHAT YOU’LL DO

As a Customer Support Representative, you’ll be on the front lines helping pool pros succeed with Skimmer. Every day, you’ll:

 - Deliver friendly, knowledgeable phone, email, and chat support to our customers

 - Guide customers through product features, troubleshooting, and best practices

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Success, and Engineering to surface feedback and resolve issues

 - Build trust through genuine empathy, clear communication, and a touch of humor (we like to have fun while we work!)

 - Become a Skimmer expert — not just knowing how the product works, but why it matters to the pros who depend on it

Your work won’t just solve problems — it’ll help shape how thousands of businesses operate more efficiently every day.

 


🧩 WHAT YOU BRING

 - A knack for clear, confident, and friendly communication

 - Experience in customer support, technical support, or billing support

 - Strong multitasking and prioritization skills — you stay calm under pressure

 - Tech-savviness — you pick up new tools quickly and love helping others do the same

 - Availability to work Monday–Friday, 9:30am - 6:00PM CDT

 


🧰 TOOLS WE USE

Experience with these is a plus (but not required — we’ll train you up!):

Our support team utilizes a range of tools to ensure efficient operations and seamless customer interactions:

 - Intercom: For chat support.

 - Dialpad: For handling calls.

 - HubSpot: CRM and comprehensive customer tracking.

 - Notion: For knowledge sharing and documentation.

 - Stripe and QBO: For managing billing and payments.

 - Slack: For facilitating internal communications.

 


💙 OUR VALUES

At Skimmer, we:

 - Realize that our customers’ success is our success

 - Build humble, diverse teams who have fun winning together

 - Drive results with urgency and ownership

 - Earn trust through transparency and honest communication

These aren’t just words — they guide how we show up every day for our customers and each other.

 


💧 WHY YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE

 - A mission-driven company with genuine customer love

 - A collaborative, ego-free culture that celebrates progress over perfection

 - Opportunities to grow and advance as Skimmer scales

 - The chance to help thousands of pool pros run smoother, smarter businesses

 - A leadership team that values clarity, empathy, and follow-through


BENEFITS

 - Competitive base pay + bonus potential

 - Generous medical, dental, and vision plans (we pay 100% of your premium and 50% of your dependents’)

 - Fantastic culture with a very strong eNPS- we work hard, celebrate wins, and learn as we go to always be improving to benefit our customers.

 - Immediate access to 401(k) with company match

 - Flexible PTO (MINIMUM of 10 days required every year)

 - 12 weeks paid parental leave for birthing parent, 6 weeks paid leave for supporting partner

 - Remote work friendly

 - Comprehensive learning and development budget

 - A manager dedicated to your development



At Skimmer we are deeply committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace (it’s in our values). We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer, seeking to identify and onboard people from all walks of life. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender ident

notes: Private equity-backed SaaS company with a strong focus on product development and customer support.
